consent.notes: All records are fully synthetic and generated for the Data Portability Hackathon 2026. No real individuals are represented.

insights.title: Stress and discretionary spend pattern
insights.finding: Weak positive relationship between stress and discretionary spend using `prior_week_stress_raw` alignment (statistically significant).
evidence: Track discretionary categories weekly and compare against stressful calendar periods.

calendar_events.title: Skate session with roommates (2h) - East Austin

spike_weeks.evidence: Week 2024-19 spend $214.50 crossed threshold $34.39; prior week stress was 0.04.

insights.what_this_means: Your spending peaks are likely linked to stressful weeks.
recommended_next_actions: Review the highest-spend week and pre-plan one lower-cost alternative activity.
Block one recovery hour in the prior week when calendar stress is high.

insights.title: Recurring anxiety themes
insights.finding: No recurring anxiety theme was detected from current conversation tags.
evidence: Themes are extracted from tagged conversation records only.
insights.what_this_means: These themes explain where emotional load is most persistent.
recommended_next_actions: Choose one recurring theme and schedule a concrete mitigation task this week.
Use weekly check-ins to track whether theme frequency is dropping.

insights.title: Savings goal velocity
insights.finding: Savings velocity cannot be computed from the current profile fields.
insights.what_this_means: Add structured savings fields in profile data to unlock this projection.
recommended_next_actions: Set a weekly transfer amount and automate it on payday.
Audit one discretionary category to increase monthly net savings.

insights.title: Freelance rate risk
insights.finding: No undercharging signal detected in the cached email data.
insights.what_this_means: Your pricing floor may be below sustainable market rates.
recommended_next_actions: Set a minimum acceptable hourly floor before sending the next quote.
Package scope in tiers so clients can trade scope instead of rate.
